calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-10-2021 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Determine Beneficiaries Of Decedens Will
Off Calendar May-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Seth Watkins appeared by telephone for Walter Leslie.

The Court waives defect in notice of the 17200 petition, and informed Mr. Watkins that a legal description of the property is missing and that a new proposed order that includes a legal description is needed. The Court stated that it will e-mail a copy of a modified order so that Mr. Watkins can provide a clean order, that it is waiving the requirement to file the original bank statements and the declaration by the attorney or personal representative certifying that the originals will be retained and that the Attorney or the Personal Representative should maintain those records for the required amount of time. Mr. Watkins stated that the house and the personal property are the only things in the estate, that there are no original bank statements and that he believes the statements are online only. The Petition to Determine Beneficiaries of Decedent's Will, and the First and Final Account, Report, and Petition for Settlement of Estate, etc. are under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petitions upon receipt of the revised orders.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Continued To Mar-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Seth Watkins for and Walter Leslie.

Mr. Watkins stated that he was just associated in to assist Attorney Bill Morris, that he was not aware that they had not responded to the Probate Examiner's notes and that it was his understanding that they had filed an amended petition. Mr. Watkins also stated that both the Attorney and the Client had health issues, and that he can review the file and make sure the questions are attended to. The Court noted that there was no appearance at the December 18th hearing, and informed Mr. Watkins that there is still an issue with notice and the issue of information concerning the relationships of the heirs. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to March 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Continued To Jan-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The matter was called at 10:15 a.m. No one was present. The Court noted that there has been no response to the Probate Examiner's notes which relate to the petitions non-conformance of the local rules with respect to the relationships of potential heirs. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Petitioner and the Attorney to appear on January 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they have not responded to the Probate Examiner's notes. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.
